Functional Modules.Function Description: Receives power from a USB-C source, provides reverse polarity protection, and regulates the input voltage to a stable system voltage rail for all internal circuitry.
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: High-Side Current/Power Sensing Module, Linear Voltage Regulator.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: Power enters via the USB-C connector. A protection circuit (e.g., using a P-MOSFET) prevents damage from incorrect connections. The raw VBUS is then fed to the sensing stage and to a linear regulator (e.g., 3.3V or 5V LDO) to create a stable, clean voltage for the microcontroller, display, and sensing IC's digital logic.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Measures the voltage drop across a precision shunt resistor to calculate the current flowing to the load, and measures the bus voltage, providing digitized data for power calculations.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: Raw power from USB-C Input, voltage across the 8mΩ shunt resistor.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: A dedicated current/power monitor IC (e.g., INA219, INA226) is placed in the high-side path. It measures the small differential voltage across the precision 8mΩ shunt resistor to calculate current, and measures the bus voltage relative to ground. The IC internally digitizes these values and makes them available via an I2C interface.

Functional Modules.Function Description: The central processing unit that reads sensor data, calculates real-time voltage, current, and power, manages the display output, and handles user input from tactile switches.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: Sensing Module (I2C SDA, SCL), User Buttons (GPIO inputs).
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: OLED Display Module (I2C SDA, SCL), Internal logic for calculations and mode control.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: An ATtiny85 microcontroller runs the main firmware. It periodically queries the sensing IC via I2C for voltage and current data. It performs calculations (e.g., Power = V * I) and manages the system state. User buttons connected to its GPIO pins are polled or use interrupts to trigger actions like resetting energy counters or cycling display modes (e.g., V/I/P/Energy).

Functional Modules.Function Description: Provides a visual interface to show real-time measurements (voltage, current, power), accumulated energy, and system status/mode.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: A small OLED display (e.g., SSD1306 based, 128x64) is connected to the same I2C bus as the sensing IC. The microcontroller sends formatted text and graphics commands to the display driver to update the readings in real-time.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Provides a clean, sensed power output to the device under test (load) and includes necessary filtering.
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: External load (via USB-C or terminal connectors).
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: This is the output stage after the shunt resistor and sensing IC. It may include output capacitors for stability and low-ESR filtering. The power is delivered to a USB-C receptacle or screw terminals to connect the load, completing the pass-through measurement path.
